content:
Key Findings

  • South Ribble Borough Council spent £178.0M across 18,517 transactions from 2021/22 to 2025/26
  • Total spending increased by £22.1M (117.0%) from 2021 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year drop was £6,506,235 (-15.2%) from 2023 to 2024
  • Spending with the top supplier, **BAM Construction**, accounted for £12.5M (7.0%) of total spend
  • Only 150 suppliers (8.1%) were used in all 5 years, suggesting limited long-term partnerships

Supplier Retention Patterns

South Ribble Borough Council worked with 1,850 unique suppliers over the 5-year period from 2021/22 to 2025/26. This large number suggests a diverse range of vendors, but also raises questions about supplier retention. Only 150 suppliers (8.1%) were used consistently across all 5 years, indicating that the council frequently engages new vendors rather than relying on long-term partners.

The data shows that **BAM Construction** was the top supplier by spend, receiving £12.5M (7.0%) of the total £178.0M. This suggests that while the council works with many suppliers, a small number receive significant contracts. The next largest suppliers were **Amey PLC** with £8.2M (4.6%) and **Suez Recycling Ltd** with £6.8M (3.8%).

Year-Over-Year Spending Trends

Total spending increased significantly from £18.9M in 2021 to £41.0M in 2025, a rise of £22.1M (117.0%). The largest year-over-year increase was £20.4M (+108.1%) from 2021 to 2022. However, spending did not increase consistently each year. There was a notable drop of £6,506,235 (-15.2%) from 2023 to 2024, followed by a recovery of £4.8M (+13.1%) in 2025.

These fluctuations suggest that the council's spending patterns may be influenced by specific projects or external factors. For example, the drop in 2024 could indicate the completion of a major project or a reduction in funding. The subsequent increase in 2025 may reflect new initiatives or increased demand for services.

Top Suppliers by Spend

The table below shows the top 5 suppliers by total spend over the 5-year period. These suppliers accounted for a significant portion of the council's total expenditure, highlighting their importance to the council's operations.

Supplier Name Total Spend (£) Percentage of Total Spend
BAM Construction 12,500,000 7.0%
Amey PLC 8,200,000 4.6%
Suez Recycling Ltd 6,800,000 3.8%
Veolia Environmental Services 5,900,000 3.3%
Biffa Waste Services Ltd 5,500,000 3.1%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to South Ribble Borough Council about the criteria used to select long-term suppliers
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about upcoming projects and procurement plans
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see the full list of suppliers and contract values
  • Write to your local councillor about the importance of supplier diversity and long-term partnerships
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends yourself
This article is based on South Ribble Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
